---
title: "butterworth_sos"
generated: true
description: "Design a digital Butterworth filter as second-order sections."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.signal.filter`](../nitrix-signal-filter.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.signal._iir`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:butterworth_sos -->
```python
butterworth_sos(*, order: int, fs: float = 1.0, btype: _BType, lo: Optional[float] = None, hi: Optional[float] = None) -> np.ndarray
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:butterworth_sos -->
Design a digital Butterworth filter as second-order sections.

Returns the `(n_sections, 6)` SOS array with `[b0, b1, b2, a0, a1, a2]`
per row -- the same layout as `scipy.signal.butter(output='sos')` (and
matching its transfer function to machine precision), implemented in
pure NumPy.  `order`, `fs` and the cut-offs are static, so the result
is a host (NumPy) constant -- the *design* is not traced; only the
*application* ([`sosfilt`](../nitrix-signal-filter/sosfilt.qmd) / [`sosfiltfilt`](../nitrix-signal-filter/sosfiltfilt.qmd)) runs in JAX.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:butterworth_sos -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `order` | `int` | — | — | Butterworth order. `'bandpass'` / `'bandstop'` produce `order` sections (`2 * order` poles); `'lowpass'` / `'highpass'` produce `ceil(order / 2)`. |
| `fs` | `float` | — | `1.0` | Sampling frequency in Hz (`1 / TR`). |
| `btype` | `_BType` | — | — | `'lowpass'` / `'highpass'` / `'bandpass'` / `'bandstop'`. |
| `lo` | `Optional[float]` | — | `None` | Cut-offs in Hz (`0 < f < fs/2`). `'lowpass'` uses `hi`; `'highpass'` uses `lo`; band types use both (`lo < hi`). |
| `hi` | `Optional[float]` | — | `None` | Cut-offs in Hz (`0 < f < fs/2`). `'lowpass'` uses `hi`; `'highpass'` uses `lo`; band types use both (`lo < hi`). |

**Returns** `np.ndarray`. The `(n_sections, 6)` second-order-section coefficient array, each row laid out as `[b0, b1, b2, a0, a1, a2]`, as a host NumPy constant.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
